turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, my internet has stopped working, so could you please help me to require it?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! I can help with that. Could you tell me what kind of internet connection you have — is it Wi-Fi, wired broadband, or mobile data?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Wi-Fi, please, in my apartment.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. Is the issue that your Wi-Fi network isn’t showing up, or it’s showing but you can’t connect to the internet?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Actually, I can connect with it and it shows in my screen, but I can't use it to check the view, no, check the moves, or send the message or picture.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, so you’re connected to Wi-Fi, but there’s no internet access. Have you tried restarting your router or modem yet?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Oh, I was afraid to restarting it because I don't know the correct connector. Yeah, last time my partner do it and he use a lot of time to use FaceTime to communicate with other workers and start it successfully. I was afraid if I do it, I can't do it.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I understand, no worries — it’s good you’re being careful. Could you tell me which company provides your internet service? That’ll help me guide you safely through the right steps.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Oh, I know, Telestra.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks! Telstra, got it. Do you happen to know where your Telstra modem or router is placed in your apartment — like near the TV, in the hallway, or another room?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Em adas domos. In my partner's rooms, in my platform's rooms, but now he moved so I can go into it and use it.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, so the modem is in your partner’s old room and now you can access it — that’s good. Could you check if the lights on the modem are on right now, and tell me what colors they are?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yellow, maybe that means it works normally.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, yellow light — that often means the modem is on but not fully connected to the internet. Could you tell me if the yellow light is steady or blinking?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Sorry, I don't know. Could you please ask a technical to my home to help me?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course. I can help you with that. Before we request a technician, could you please confirm the address where the service is installed?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, of course.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great, thanks! Could you please tell me your full address, including the street number, street name, and suburb?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Of course, I live in the Morgan, Queen Street, 115-7. And if you ask somebody to this address, please call me. My phone number is 04667891999. You can call me and I will take the lift down and lead to him.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks for sharing that. For your privacy and safety, I can’t make real service calls or contact technicians directly. But I can guide you on how to request one through Telstra. You can call Telstra support at 13 22 00 or visit telstra.com.au/support — they’ll schedule a technician for you. Would you like me to show you exactly what to say when you call them?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Please, actually I can't describe the question I faced, so please teach me what should I say when I call them.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! Here’s a simple way you can say it on the phone — nice and clear: “Hi, my Wi-Fi is connected, but there’s no internet. The modem light is yellow. I’d like to book a technician to come and check it, please.” They may then ask you for: Your name Your address (you can give 115-7 Queen Street, Morgan) Your contact number If you’ve tried restarting the modem yet Would you like me to help you practice how the conversation might go, like a short roleplay?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No, I will call them later. Thank you.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re very welcome. I hope your internet gets fixed soon. Have a good day!
